summary: Authenticated Google Workspace CLI provider for Docs, Drive, Sheets, Slides, Gmail, Calendar, and adjacent Workspace gather flows.

notes: Install `gws` through the upstream npm package or another host-approved package path.
For gather-oriented Google Workspace access, prefer `gws auth login --readonly -s drive,docs,sheets,slides` after install.
If no OAuth client is configured yet, use `gws auth setup --login` first.
Update through the same mechanism that originally installed `gws`.

detect.failure_hint: Install `gws` first or use a stronger host-provided Google Workspace capability path.

healthcheck.failure_hint: The CLI exists but its auth surface is unavailable. Reinstall `gws` or fall back to a different Google Workspace provider path.

readiness_checks.summary: Google Workspace CLI has an authenticated configuration available.
readiness_checks.failure_hint: Run `gws auth login --readonly -s drive,docs,sheets,slides` or provide the approved env fallback before relying on Google Workspace gather.

config_layers.summary: Prefer an already authenticated `gws` session for private Google Workspace gather.
Fall back to the documented `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_*` environment variables in ordinary local operator setups.
When no auth state exists yet, the operator may need `gws auth setup --login` or `gws auth login` before gather can proceed.

version_expectation.constraint: Prefer a recent upstream-supported gws release.

notes: Upstream documents `npm install -g @googleworkspace/cli` as the package-manager install path.
Upstream documents `brew install googleworkspace-cli` on macOS and Linux.

when_missing: Do not claim private Google Workspace access succeeded without an authenticated CLI or a stronger host capability path.
Ask for an exported artifact or a different source when the required Workspace capability is absent.
when_version_mismatch: Treat schema drift or API-surface mismatch as a manual review point before trusting gathered Google Workspace content.

host_notes: This manifest models the `gws` CLI path only. A future host-provided Google Workspace grant can sit alongside it as a stronger provider path.
The binary is the ownership boundary; `charness` should not reintroduce a copied Google public-export helper path.
